questions.question: What does each router maintain in a link-state protocol?
options: Only its directly connected neighbors and their costs
A complete map of the network topology
The distance vectors advertised by all neighbors
Only the shortest path to the nearest exit router
questions.explanation: In link-state routing, every router floods its link information so that all routers build an identical complete topology map (link-state database).

questions.question: What algorithm does each router run on its link-state database?
options: Bellman-Ford
Dijkstra's shortest-path-first (SPF)
Ford-Fulkerson maximum flow
Kruskal's minimum spanning tree
questions.explanation: Each link-state router independently runs Dijkstra's algorithm on its LSDB to compute the shortest path tree rooted at itself.

questions.question: Why does link-state routing converge faster than distance vector?
options: It uses shorter update timers
After one round of LSA flooding, each router computes optimal paths locally — no iterative relaxation needed
It requires fewer routers to participate in the protocol
It only computes paths to directly connected neighbors
questions.explanation: LS converges after one flood round because every router has the complete topology and runs Dijkstra locally. DV needs multiple rounds of iterative relaxation proportional to the network diameter.

questions.question: What is the primary criterion BGP uses to select routes between autonomous systems?
options: Lowest link cost (like OSPF)
Shortest AS path and policy attributes (local_pref, communities)
Highest bandwidth available
Round-trip time measured by ping
questions.explanation: BGP is policy-driven. Route selection considers local_pref first, then shortest AS path length, then MED, with many other tiebreakers. Commercial relationships, not just metrics, drive inter-AS routing.

questions.question: What is BGP and why is it called the 'glue of the internet'?
options: A protocol for transferring files between servers
The Border Gateway Protocol that routes traffic between autonomous systems (ISPs, large networks)
A protocol for assigning IP addresses
A local area network protocol
questions.explanation: BGP is the inter-domain routing protocol that connects autonomous systems (ASes) — ISPs, cloud providers, enterprises. Each AS announces its IP prefixes to neighbors, and BGP selects paths based on policy (not just shortest path). It literally holds the internet together.
